name: shell_exec
description: Execute a shell command. Supports cd: you can write 'cd /path && git status' and it will run in that directory. You can also set working_dir directly. Timeout: 60s default, 120s for git push/pull/fetch/clone. Allowlisted commands: cd, git, ls, cat, grep, find, curl, node, python3, npm, echo, pwd, date, qmd, which, head, tail, wc, mkdir, cp, mv, rsync, sed, awk, sort, uniq, diff, tar, jq, touch, chmod, tr, cut, paste, xargs, basename, dirname, realpath, kill, pkill, ps, lsof.

command.description: Shell command to run. Supports 'cd /path && command' — the cd path becomes the working directory. Bare 'cd /path' returns a directory listing.

working_dir.description: Working directory to run the command in. Equivalent to cd'ing first. Defaults to home directory if not set. Use this or 'cd /path && ...' in the command — both work.
